task_completion.description: Generate a 3D surface of the skull from the loaded DICOM scan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D model of a human skull is visible in the main viewport of the medical imaging software.

task_completion.description: Digitally extract the lower incisors and canines (approximately 6 front teeth).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D view of the skull, the lower front teeth (incisors and canines) are visibly missing, erased, or cropped out.

task_completion.description: Ensure the mandibular bone remains intact after teeth removal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bone of the lower jaw (mandible) beneath the removed teeth is still present and structurally intact, not completely hollowed out or deleted.

task_completion.description: Export a screenshot showing the front of the mandible to /home/ga/Documents/edentulous_view.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager window, or image viewer confirming the creation of 'edentulous_view.png' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: Export the modified 3D surface as an STL file to /home/ga/Documents/edentulous_skull.stl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager window confirming the creation of 'edentulous_skull.stl' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the medical imaging software's GUI to generate and edit the 3D model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the software's interface (e.g., InVesalius), such as clicking buttons, adjusting thresholds, or using 3D tools, rather than relying solely on terminal scripts.

integrity.description: The agent actively edited the model to remove the teeth rather than loading a pre-modified file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visibility of editing tools being used, such as mask editing, cropping boxes, or surface erasure tools applied specifically to the lower anterior teeth region.

integrity.description: The agent exported the files using the software's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The software's 'Export 3D surface' or 'Save Screenshot' dialog boxes are visible, ensuring the files are not just empty dummy files created via terminal commands like 'touch'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output paths are exactly '/home/ga/Documents/edentulous_skull.stl' and '/home/ga/Documents/edentulous_view.png'. The exported STL file must contain at least 10,000 triangles to be considered a valid 3D surface.
